assertions.description: 62 total components across parent+child sheets, all refs properly grouped in BOM. 22 decoupling caps on VCC correctly identified with bulk (22uF) and bypass (100nF) coverage. 5 RC filters (200Ω + 330pF, 2.41 MHz) correctly detected — these are SCSI termination networks.

The parent sheet uses J1 for the Apple II bus edge connector (lib: PCM_MA_Connector_AppleII:Bus_Apple_II_Card_Side) and the child sheet main_logic also uses J1 for a SW_DIP_x08. These are genuinely two different physical components sharing the same reference designator — a real design annotation error that the analyzer correctly flags.

Bus detection is accurate for this SCSI card design. Hierarchical label set and bus signals are consistent with an Apple II SCSI card interfacing the Apple II address bus (A0-A10), data bus (D0-D7), buffered SCSI data (B_D0-B_D7), and extended address (EA8-EA13). The bus 'width' values (e.g., 45 for A prefix) represent entry counts across both sheets, not signal count.
